18	
    19	## Current phase
    20	
    21	**Phase 6 complete.** The `eden-storage` package ships the `Store`
    22	structural interface for chapter 8 — task store + event log +
    23	proposal/trial persistence collapsed into a single Protocol — and
    24	two conforming backends: `InMemoryStore` (fast, non-durable; moved
    25	from `eden-dispatch`) and `SqliteStore` (durable across restarts via
    26	a WAL-mode SQLite database). Both share the transition logic in
    27	`_base.py`, and the same conformance scenarios are parametrized
    28	across both backends — drift from the Protocol surfaces in tests,
    29	not in production. Restart-safety tests close and reopen a SQLite
    30	store mid-experiment to confirm state, event log, and claim tokens
    31	survive, and a monkey-patched commit failure verifies rollback.
    32	Phase 7 is next: the reference git integrator (`eden-git`). See
    33	[`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) for the full 13-phase plan.
    34	
    35	## Commands
    36	
    37	At Phase 6, markdown linting, JSON Schema validation, and the Python
    38	toolchain for the `eden-contracts`, `eden-dispatch`, and `eden-storage`
    39	reference packages are wired up.
    40

70	### Shared transition logic (`_base.py`)
    71	
    72	- One `_StoreBase` holds every public method, every validation
    73	  helper, and every composite-commit staging path. Subclasses
    74	  override only the backend primitives (`_get_task`,
    75	  `_iter_trials`, `_atomic_operation`, `_apply_commit`, …).
    76	- This is the structural guarantee that "both backends pass the
    77	  same conformance suite" actually means something: there is only
    78	  one code path for the state machine and the composite commits;
    79	  a future Postgres backend or a remote backend that overrides the
    80	  primitives inherits every invariant for free.
    81	
    82	### SQLite backend (`sqlite.py` + `_schema.py`)
    83	
    84	- Each public op: `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` → run the shared logic →
    85	  `COMMIT` on normal exit, `ROLLBACK` on exception. `_in_txn`
    86	  tracks re-entry so helper methods called from inside a public
    87	  op (e.g. `_accept_implement`) participate in the outer
    88	  transaction rather than opening a nested one.
    89	- Row layout: one table per entity kind; `data` column is a JSON
    90	  blob of the full Pydantic dump; denormalized columns (`kind`,
    91	  `state`, `status`) index filtered queries only. Rehydration goes
    92	  back through `Model.model_validate_json` so the denormalized
    93	  columns cannot silently diverge from the canonical JSON (drift
    94	  would surface at the next read).
    95	- PRAGMAs: `foreign_keys = ON`, `journal_mode = WAL`,
    96	  `synchronous = NORMAL`. WAL + NORMAL is the standard durability
    97	  point for a single-process reference impl; chapter 8 §3.1
    98	  requires "survives a subsequent crash of the store's host", which
    99	  WAL satisfies.
   100	- Migrations are linear. `_MIGRATIONS` is a list of callables
   101	  indexed by version; `ensure_schema` applies every pending one
   102	  under a single outer transaction. Adding a future migration means
   103	  appending a function.
   104	- The default `event_id` counter in `_StoreBase` starts at 1 every
   105	  time a store object is constructed. On reopen that would produce
   106	  duplicate `event_id` values and violate the table's UNIQUE
   107	  constraint. `SqliteStore.__init__` resumes the counter from the
   108	  persisted max when the caller didn't supply their own factory
   109	  (caller-supplied factories are responsible for their own
   110	  collision avoidance).
   111	- `_initialize_experiment` enforces chapter 8 §4.2 (metrics-schema
   112	  immutability): on reopen, the stored `experiment_id` must match
   113	  the requested one, and a supplied `metrics_schema` must match
   114	  the persisted JSON. Reopen without a schema argument inherits the
   115	  persisted one so §4.3 metric validation keeps running.
   116	
   117	### Conformance across backends
   118	
   119	- `tests/conftest.py` parametrizes `make_store` across
   120	  (`InMemoryStore`, `SqliteStore`). Every conformance scenario
   121	  (`test_task_state_machine.py`, `test_composite_commits.py`,
   122	  `test_store_hardening.py`) now runs twice — 110 scenarios total,
   123	  both backends green.
   124	- The 9 `test_restart_safety.py` tests are `SqliteStore`-only
   125	  because the scenario (close + reopen) is meaningless for an
   126	  in-memory backend. They cover: persistence of every entity +
   127	  event on reopen; claim-token survival; total-order preservation
   128	  across a restart boundary; rejection of reopen under a different
   129	  experiment id or metrics schema; inheritance of persisted metrics
   130	  schema; rollback-on-exception via a monkey-patched commit
   131	  failure; full planner→implementer→{restart}→evaluator→integrator
   132	  cycle.
   133	
   134	## Pre-review audit (five impl-review lenses)
   135	
   136	Applied the `feedback_impl_review_lenses.md` audit:
   137	
   138	1. **Mutable-model boundary leak.** Every `read_*` / `list_*` /
   139	   `events()` in `_base.py` deep-copies. `create_proposal`,
   140	   `create_trial`, and `submit` deep-copy caller-supplied values
   141	   before persisting. `SqliteStore` reads additionally rehydrate
   142	   fresh Pydantic models from JSON; the `_deep` wrapper is then a
   143	   no-op-equivalent belt-and-suspenders.
   144	2. **`model_copy(update=...)` validator bypass.** No call sites.
   145	   `grep -rn "model_copy(update" reference/` returns only the
   146	   docstring that explains why we avoid it. All updates go through
   147	   `_validated_update` which dumps → merges → `model_validate`.
   148	3. **Validation failures terminalize, not raise.** Preserved from
   149	   Phase 5. `validate_terminal` returns a three-way decision; the
   150	   driver routes to accept / reject(worker_error) /
   151	   reject(validation_error).
   152	4. **Status vs. reason keying asymmetry.** Preserved. `_reject_evaluate`
   153	   keys trial-side transitions on `submission.status`, not on the
   154	   orchestrator's `reason`.
   155	5. **Aspirational framing in the brief.** Grep-verified every
   156	   claim:
   157	   - "only one code path for the state machine" — grep confirms
   158	     no duplication of transition logic in `memory.py` or
   159	     `sqlite.py`; both only override the primitive hooks.
   160	   - "WAL + NORMAL satisfies §3.1" — this is a claim about SQLite
   161	     behavior, not about my code. The test asserts close/reopen
   162	     preserves every acknowledged write; the WAL claim is
   163	     conventional and is the conventional interpretation of the
   164	     spec's "survives a subsequent crash" language.
   165	   - "backends inherit every invariant for free" — verified by
   166	     parametrizing 55 conformance scenarios across both backends;
   167	     both pass without any scenario-side accommodation.
   168	
   169	## Notable design decisions to audit
   170	
   171	1. **Single `Store` Protocol vs. three Protocols.** Chapter 8
   172	   names three stores; the Protocol merges them. Rationale in
   173	   `protocol.py` module docstring: composite commits span store
   174	   boundaries, and a Protocol split across three objects would
   175	   need an external commit coordinator (added complexity, no
   176	   normative benefit). A reviewer may argue the opposite — that
   177	   the Protocol should mirror the spec's noun structure — and I
   178	   would want to hear it.
   179	2. **Re-entrant transactions.** `_in_txn` allows `_accept_implement`
   180	   etc. to call `_apply_commit` without opening a second
   181	   transaction. The alternative is to split every public op into a
   182	   "validate + stage" phase and a "commit" phase and have only the
   183	   public op open the transaction. The re-entrant approach
   184	   preserves the existing in-memory code structure (helpers are
   185	   `_apply_commit`-callable in both backends), but it does mean the
   186	   "one BEGIN per public op" rule is enforced by discipline, not
   187	   structurally.
   188	3. **`_max_event_seq` sniffs only the default `evt-NNNNNN` format.**
   189	   A caller-supplied `event_id_factory` is responsible for its own
   190	   collision avoidance on reopen; I don't try to interpret arbitrary
   191	   event_id patterns. This is a reasonable boundary because the
   192	   factory is caller-controlled, but a reviewer may prefer that I
   193	   persist the counter explicitly (e.g. in the `experiment` row)
   194	   rather than inferring it from max(event_id).
   195	4. **WAL + `synchronous = NORMAL`.** Industry-standard durability
   196	   for a reference impl. `synchronous = FULL` would give crash
   197	   safety against kernel panics / power loss; `NORMAL` gives crash
   198	   safety against process death, which is the scenario chapter 8
   199	   §3.1 is worded around. A reviewer who interprets §3.1 as
   200	   requiring fsync-on-commit may want `FULL`.
   201	5. **No Postgres, no blob store.** Per Phase 6 non-goals. I want
   202	   this confirmed rather than assumed — please flag if chapter 8
   203	   §5 (artifact store) is close enough to a storage-chapter
   204	   deliverable that Phase 6 should have shipped a stub.
   205	
   206	## Scope this review should cover
   207	
   208	1. Does the Protocol faithfully express chapter 8 §1 + §1.7 + §2?
   209	2. Does `SqliteStore` satisfy chapter 8 §3 (durability + crash
   210	   recovery) under the reading that a process crash is the
   211	   unit test's close/reopen boundary, given WAL + `synchronous =
   212	   NORMAL`?
   213	3. Does every composite commit in `05-event-protocol.md` §2.2
   214	   actually land atomically in SQLite — i.e. either all rows of
   215	   a `_Tx` persist, or none do, including on exceptions raised
   216	   mid-commit? The monkey-patched test asserts this for one such
   217	   commit; are there composites I should also spot-check?
   218	4. Is the re-entrant transaction model correct, or does it hide a
   219	   bug where a helper's `_apply_commit` could succeed while the
   220	   outer transaction is already doomed?
   221	5. Is the `_max_event_seq` reconstruction approach defensible, or
   222	   should the counter live in its own persisted cell?
   223	6. Any corners of chapter 8 the refactor missed (event log
   224	   retention §2.4, no-fabrication §3.4, artifact-store §5
   225	   boundary)?

1	# eden-storage
     2	
     3	Reference storage backends for the EDEN protocol
     4	([`spec/v0/08-storage.md`](../../../spec/v0/08-storage.md)).
     5	
     6	This package defines the [`Store`][store-protocol] structural interface
     7	— the union of the task store, event log, and proposal/trial
     8	persistence that chapter 8 §1, §1.7, and §2 specify — and ships two
     9	backends that satisfy it:
    10	
    11	- **`InMemoryStore`** (lives in
    12	  [`eden-dispatch`](../eden-dispatch/), re-exported from here for
    13	  convenience) — single-process, non-durable, suitable for tests and
    14	  the Phase 5 dispatch loop.
    15	- **`SqliteStore`** — single-process, SQLite-backed, **durable**
    16	  across process restarts. The smallest backend that satisfies
    17	  chapter 8 §3 (durability, read-after-write, crash recovery).
    18	
    19	Both backends pass the same conformance scenarios
    20	([`tests/`](tests/)); adding a third backend (Postgres, Gitea-adjacent,
    21	…) is a matter of implementing the Protocol and running the suite.
    22	
    23	[store-protocol]: src/eden_storage/protocol.py
    24	
    25	## Scope (Phase 6)
    26	
    27	- One Protocol, two backends, shared conformance.
    28	- SQLite schema + migrations under
    29	  [`_schema.py`](src/eden_storage/_schema.py).
    30	- Restart-safety tests: close and reopen the SQLite store mid-experiment
    31	  and assert all state and events survive.
    32	
    33	Non-goals at this phase:
    34	
    35	- No Postgres backend (Phase 12).
    36	- No artifact store (Phase 10).
    37	- No cross-process transport (Phase 8 owns the wire protocol).
    38	- No role-scoped handles — a caller with access to the store can call
    39	  any mutation method; role negative rules are enforced by the
    40	  conformance suite (Phase 11), not by the storage layer.

30	
    31	### 1.1 Operations
    32	
    33	| Operation | Arguments | Effect |
    34	|---|---|---|
    35	| `create_task` | fully-formed `task` object with `state == "pending"` | Atomically inserts the task and appends `task.created` to the event log ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)). |
    36	| `claim` | `task_id`, `worker_id`, OPTIONAL `expires_at` | Atomically transitions a `pending` task to `claimed`, issues a fresh claim token, and appends `task.claimed`. |
    37	| `submit` | `task_id`, `token`, role-specific result | Atomically transitions a `claimed` task to `submitted`, persists the result, and appends `task.submitted`. Idempotent per [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.2. |
    38	| `accept` | `task_id`, role-specific acceptance payload | Atomically transitions `submitted → completed`, applies the role-specific effect (e.g. proposal completion, trial status write), clears `claim`, and appends `task.completed` plus any composite events. |
    39	| `reject` | `task_id`, `reason`, role-specific failure payload | Atomically transitions `submitted → failed`, applies the role-specific effect, clears `claim`, and appends `task.failed` plus any composite events. |
    40	| `reclaim` | `task_id`, OPTIONAL `cause` | Atomically transitions `claimed`/`submitted` back to `pending` (where permitted; [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §5.1), invalidates the token, clears `claim`, performs §2.2 composite effects where applicable, and appends `task.reclaimed`. |
    41	| `read_task` | `task_id` | Returns the current task object or a well-defined not-found signal. |
    42	| `list_tasks` | filter (`kind`, `state`, `experiment_id`, …) | Returns matching tasks. Ordering is implementation-defined. |
    43	
    44	"Atomically" means: the state change, the result-payload write, any
    45	cross-entity effect listed in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md)
    46	§2.2, and the event appends are all durable together or none of them
    47	is ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)).
    48	
    49	### 1.2 Atomic claim (serialization)
    50	
    51	For any given `pending` task, at most one concurrent `claim`
    52	invocation MUST succeed. A conforming task store MUST expose this as
    53	a linearizable operation: after a successful claim, every subsequent
    54	reader of the task observes `state == "claimed"` with the new claim
    55	object; no reader observes an intermediate state without the claim.
    56	
    57	The serialization mechanism (row lock + update, compare-and-set on
    58	version, `SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`) is implementation-defined.
    59	The observable guarantee is not.
    60	
    61	### 1.3 Token-authorization rule
    62	
    63	`submit` and any other claim-holder operation MUST reject calls whose
    64	presented token does not equal the token currently recorded on the
    65	task's `claim` object
    66	([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §3.3). A conforming
    67	task store MUST NOT use `worker_id` as a substitute for the token
    68	when authorizing.
    69	
    70	### 1.4 Idempotent resubmit
    71	
    72	The idempotency rule for `submit` is specified normatively in
    73	[`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.2. A conforming task
    74	store MUST implement it: a resubmit carrying the current token, with
    75	a content-equivalent payload, MUST succeed without mutating state;
    76	an inconsistent resubmit MUST be rejected.
    77	
    78	### 1.5 Terminal immutability
    79	
    80	Once a task is `completed` or `failed`
    81	([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.4), no subsequent
    82	operation MAY change any task field except audit-only metadata the
    83	task store documents outside the normative schema. In particular,
    84	`reclaim` MUST be rejected on terminal tasks.
    85	
    86	### 1.6 Per-task serialization
    87	
    88	All mutating operations on a single task MUST be serialized: the
    89	observable history of a task is a total order
    90	([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §6.1). A conforming
    91	task store MUST NOT expose a state that has not yet been accompanied
    92	by its event ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)).
    93	
    94	### 1.7 Proposal and trial persistence
    95	
    96	Proposals ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5) and trials
    97	(§7) are persisted alongside tasks. A conforming deployment MAY
    98	back them with the task store itself, with a separate store, or
    99	with any combination; the protocol constrains the observable
   100	contract, not the physical layout. Every conforming deployment MUST
   101	expose, for both proposals and trials, at minimum:
   102	
   103	- **Create** — durably insert a new object whose fields validate
   104	  against the corresponding schema
   105	  ([`schemas/proposal.schema.json`](schemas/proposal.schema.json),
   106	  [`schemas/trial.schema.json`](schemas/trial.schema.json)).
   107	- **Read** — return the current object or a well-defined not-found
   108	  signal.
   109	- **List** — return objects matching a filter (by `experiment_id`,
   110	  `state`/`status`, etc.). Ordering is implementation-defined.
   111	- **Update** — apply the field writes the role contracts and task-
   112	  terminal transitions specify ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §2.2,
   113	  §3.2, §4.4; [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3,
   114	  §5.4).
   115	
   116	All proposal and trial writes MUST commit atomically with the
   117	accompanying event(s) per §6. The durability (§3.1), read-after-write
   118	(§3.2), crash-recovery (§3.3), and no-fabrication (§3.4) rules apply
   119	uniformly. Terminal immutability applies to proposals in
   120	`completed` and trials in `success`/`error`/`eval_error` — the
   121	`trial_commit_sha` field is the **one** post-terminal write
   122	permitted on a trial, written exclusively by the integrator
   123	([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §3.4), and it MUST be
   124	written atomically with its event and its `trial/*` ref.
   125	
   126	## 2. Event log
   127	
   128	The event log holds the per-experiment event stream. It MUST support
   129	the operations below and MUST honor the delivery guarantees specified
   130	in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.
   131	
   132	### 2.1 Operations
   133	
   134	| Operation | Arguments | Effect |
   135	|---|---|---|
   136	| `append` | fully-formed event object | Atomically appends the event, together with the state change it describes ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)). |
   137	| `read_range` | experiment_id, OPTIONAL cursor | Returns a durable, ordered range of events. |
   138	| `subscribe` | experiment_id, OPTIONAL starting cursor | Returns a stream that delivers events in log order until cancelled. |
   139	| `replay` | experiment_id | Returns the full stream from the first event ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.4). |
   140	
   141	`append` MUST NOT be exposed as a standalone operation that the task
   142	store, proposal store, or trial store can call independently of the
   143	state change the event describes. The atomicity contract
   144	([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)) requires the append to travel
   145	with the state change; the event log operation is a primitive of the
   146	composite transaction, not an independent one.
   147	
   148	### 2.2 Ordering and causality
   149	
   150	The log MUST present a single total order per experiment
   151	([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.1) that respects
   152	causality ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.2). The
   153	ordering mechanism is implementation-defined; the observable
   154	guarantee is not.
   155	
   156	The log MAY provide a global ordering across experiments as an
   157	optimization, but MUST NOT require subscribers to depend on it
   158	([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.6).
   159	
   160	### 2.3 Delivery
   161	
   162	At-least-once delivery is the minimum contract
   163	([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.3).
   164	Implementations MAY offer exactly-once via durable offset commits.
   165	
   166	### 2.4 Retention
   167	
   168	For the lifetime of an experiment — from registration until the
   169	experiment reaches its operator-declared terminal state — a
   170	conforming event log MUST retain **every** appended event for that
   171	experiment and MUST serve full replay from the first event
   172	([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.4). No
   173	compaction, archival, or deletion MAY occur inside this window. A
   174	deployment MAY compact or archive an experiment's events once it
   175	has reached its operator-declared terminal state; after compaction
   176	the log MUST either (a) reject replays older than the compaction
   177	horizon with a well-defined error, or (b) serve those replays from
   178	an archive that preserves order and content. It MUST NOT silently
   179	drop events from a replay.
   180	
   181	## 3. Durability
   182	
   183	The following applies uniformly to the task store and the event log.
   184	
   185	### 3.1 Write durability
   186	
   187	An operation that the store acknowledges to its caller MUST survive
   188	a subsequent crash of the store's host. "Acknowledgement" is the
   189	return value indicating success; a store MUST NOT return success for
   190	a write that has not yet been persisted to durable media (or the

item.text: There’s one more accuracy check worth settling: the brief says migrations run “under a single outer transaction,” but `_schema.ensure_schema()` never issues `BEGIN`. I’m probing the actual sqlite3 behavior before I call that claim false.

    72	"""
    73	
    74	
    75	def _apply_v1(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> None:
    76	    conn.executescript(_MIGRATION_V1)
    77	
    78	
    79	_MIGRATIONS: list[Callable[[sqlite3.Connection], None]] = [_apply_v1]
    80	
    81	
    82	def current_version(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> int:
    83	    """Return the applied schema version, or 0 if uninitialized."""
    84	    row = conn.execute(
    85	        "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='schema_version'"
    86	    ).fetchone()
    87	    if row is None:
    88	        return 0
    89	    row = conn.execute("SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version").fetchone()
    90	    return row[0] or 0
    91	
    92	
    93	def ensure_schema(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> None:
    94	    """Apply every pending migration, in order, under one transaction.
    95	
    96	    Idempotent: re-running on an up-to-date database is a no-op.
    97	    """
    98	    # Create the version table out-of-band so we can insert into it
    99	    # without itself needing to be in _MIGRATIONS (which would be a
   100	    # circularity).
   101	    conn.executescript(
   102	        "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version "
   103	        "(version INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY);"
   104	    )
   105	    applied = current_version(conn)
   106	    target = len(_MIGRATIONS)
   107	    if applied >= target:
   108	        return
   109	    for version in range(applied + 1, target + 1):
   110	        migration = _MIGRATIONS[version - 1]
   111	        migration(conn)
   112	        conn.execute(
   113	            "INSERT INTO schema_version(version) VALUES (?)", (version,)
   114	        )
   115	    conn.commit()

/bin/zsh -lc "uv run python - <<'PY'
import sqlite3, tempfile, os
fd, path = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix='.db')
os.close(fd)
conn = sqlite3.connect(path, isolation_level=None)
try:
    try:
        conn.executescript('CREATE TABLE a(x); INSERT INTO nosuch VALUES (1);')
    except Exception as e:
        print(type(e).__name__, e)
    row = conn.execute(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='a'\").fetchone()
    print('table_a_exists', row is not None)
finally:
    conn.close()
    os.unlink(path)
PY"

220	Every experiment declares a metrics schema in its `experiment_config`
   221	([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §1.3,
   222	[`schemas/metrics-schema.schema.json`](schemas/metrics-schema.schema.json)).
   223	The task store (or an equivalently-scoped component in a conforming
   224	deployment) MUST enforce the following at every write that touches
   225	trial metrics.
   226	
   227	### 4.1 Registration
   228	
   229	At experiment registration time, a conforming deployment MUST persist
   230	the experiment's metrics schema durably and atomically with the
   231	experiment's other configuration. A subsequent write of a
   232	trial's `metrics` field MUST be validated against the schema
   233	registered for that experiment; no write MAY bypass this validation.
   234	
   235	### 4.2 Immutability during an experiment
   236	
   237	A metrics schema MUST NOT be mutated for the lifetime of the
   238	experiment. An operator who wishes to change the metrics set MUST
   239	register a new experiment (Phase 12 defines the control-plane
   240	operations that govern this, but the invariant applies from v0
   241	onward). A conforming deployment MUST reject any in-flight mutation
   242	of an existing experiment's metrics schema.
   243	
   244	The rationale is canonicality: comparing trials across an experiment
   245	only has meaning if the metric definitions they are compared on did
   246	not move during the experiment.
   247	
   248	### 4.3 Per-metric type checks
   249	
   250	A successful write of a `trial.metrics` payload MUST satisfy:
   251	
   252	- Every key in `metrics` is present in the experiment's metrics
   253	  schema.
   254	- Every value either satisfies the declared type of its key — per
   255	  the type mapping in [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §1.3
   256	  (`integer`, `real`, `text`) — or is `null`.
   257	- No reserved name ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §6.2)
   258	  appears as a key.
   259	
   260	A write that violates any of these MUST be rejected; the store MUST
   261	NOT record a trial as `success` with an invalid metrics payload
   262	([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.2).
   263	
   264	## 5. Artifact store
   265	
   266	The artifact store holds files the roles produce (plan text, code
   267	diffs, evaluator outputs, logs) and exposes them by URI. It is less
   268	load-bearing than the task store and event log — the protocol uses
   269	it only for content referenced by `artifacts_uri` on proposals and
   270	trials — and its contract is accordingly narrower.
   271	
   272	### 5.1 Operations
   273	
   274	A conforming artifact store MUST support:
   275	
   276	- **Upload** — given content bytes and a proposed identity (naming
   277	  scheme is implementation-defined), persist the bytes and return a
   278	  URI that will later resolve to them.
   279	- **Fetch** — given a URI the store issued, return the byte content
   280	  the upload persisted.
   281	
   282	URIs MUST be RFC 3986–conformant
   283	([`schemas/proposal.schema.json`](schemas/proposal.schema.json)
   284	enforces this on the proposal side).
   285	
   286	### 5.2 Durability
   287	
   288	Once the artifact store returns a URI to an uploader, the content at
   289	that URI MUST remain resolvable until the experiment's retention
   290	window elapses. A conforming deployment MUST define its retention
   291	window explicitly. A URI recorded on a `trial/*` commit's eval
   292	manifest — whether as the optional `artifacts_uri` field
   293	([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §4.2) or inside the optional
   294	per-file `artifacts` inventory
   295	([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §4.4) — MUST be resolvable
   296	for at least as long as that `trial/*` commit is retained.
   297	
   298	### 5.3 Content integrity
   299	
   300	The artifact store MUST NOT serve byte content that differs from
   301	what was originally uploaded for a given URI. A store that versions
   302	URIs MUST ensure a fetch against a given URI returns the upload that
   303	produced it, not a subsequent overwrite.
   304	
   305	When the optional per-file `artifacts` inventory
   306	([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §4.4) is present on a
   307	`trial/*` commit, the manifest's recorded `sha256` and `bytes`
   308	fields are the canonical attestation of that file's byte content at
   309	integration time; the artifact store is not required to compute or
   310	verify them at fetch time, but its no-overwrite rule is what makes
   311	the attestation load-bearing.
   312	
   313	### 5.4 No protocol-owned mutation after the fact
   314	
   315	Once a protocol-owned object (proposal, trial, eval manifest)
   316	references an artifact URI, a conforming deployment MUST NOT
   317	overwrite the content at that URI. Overwriting would break
   318	reproducibility guarantees that subscribers reading the event log
   319	rely on.
   320

193	**Units:**
   194	
   195	- **6a — complete.** `Store` Protocol in `reference/packages/eden-storage/
   196	  src/eden_storage/protocol.py` pins the chapter-8 union (task store,
   197	  event log, proposal/trial persistence). Shared transition logic lives
   198	  in `_base.py`; `InMemoryStore` (moved from `eden-dispatch`) and the
   199	  new `SqliteStore` both satisfy the Protocol and inherit the same
   200	  validation and composite-commit paths.
   201	- **6b — complete.** SQLite-backed backend in `eden_storage/sqlite.py`
   202	  with a linear-migration schema in `_schema.py`. Every public op opens
   203	  a `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction; commit on success, rollback on
   204	  exception. `WAL` + `synchronous = NORMAL` provides durability
   205	  (chapter 8 §3.1). A reopened store resumes the event-id counter
   206	  from the persisted max, inherits the metrics schema, and rejects a
   207	  reopen whose `experiment_id` or `metrics_schema` does not match.
   208	  Conformance scenarios (55 per backend) run via a parametrized
   209	  `make_store` fixture, and 9 restart-safety tests close/reopen a
   210	  SQLite store mid-experiment and verify state/events/tokens survive.
   211	  A monkey-patched commit-failure test asserts rollback.
   212	
   213	**Chunk:** 6a + 6b — one chunk, shipped together.
   214	
   215	**Non-goals:** Postgres (later); blob storage (Phase 10).
   216

180	    def _initialize_experiment(
   181	        self, experiment_id: str, metrics_schema: MetricsSchema | None
   182	    ) -> None:
   183	        """Create or validate the single experiment row.
   184	
   185	        On first open inserts the experiment + metrics_schema. On
   186	        reopen, enforces that both match what was persisted — chapter
   187	        8 §4.2 says the metrics schema MUST NOT change during an
   188	        experiment's lifetime.
   189	        """
   190	        row = self._conn.execute(
   191	            "SELECT experiment_id, metrics_schema FROM experiment"
   192	        ).fetchone()
   193	        schema_json: str | None = None
   194	        if metrics_schema is not None:
   195	            schema_json = json.dumps(metrics_schema.model_dump(mode="json"))
   196	        if row is None:
   197	            self._conn.execute(
   198	                "INSERT INTO experiment(experiment_id, metrics_schema) VALUES (?, ?)",
   199	                (experiment_id, schema_json),
   200	            )
   201	            self._conn.commit()
   202	            return
   203	        stored_id, stored_schema = row
   204	        if stored_id != experiment_id:
   205	            raise InvalidPrecondition(
   206	                f"database at {self._path!r} belongs to experiment "
   207	                f"{stored_id!r}, not {experiment_id!r}"
   208	            )
   209	        if metrics_schema is None:
   210	            # No schema supplied: inherit whatever was persisted so
   211	            # §4.3 enforcement survives restart. `None` stored + `None`
   212	            # argument is also fine — metrics simply aren't validated.
   213	            if stored_schema is not None:
   214	                self._metrics_schema = MetricsSchema.model_validate_json(stored_schema)
   215	        elif stored_schema != schema_json:
   216	            raise InvalidPrecondition(
   217	                "metrics_schema MUST NOT change for the lifetime of an "
   218	                "experiment (08-storage.md §4.2); database at "
   219	                f"{self._path!r} has a different schema recorded"
   220	            )
   221	
   222	    def _max_event_seq(self) -> int:
   223	        """Return the highest default event-id counter value ever used.
   224	
   225	        The default counter produces strings of the form
   226	        ``evt-000001``; we extract the numeric suffix. Non-matching
   227	        event_ids (for instance, values from a caller-supplied
   228	        ``event_id_factory`` pre-Phase-6) are ignored, because
   229	        caller-supplied factories are responsible for their own
   230	        collision avoidance.
   231	        """
   232	        row = self._conn.execute(
   233	            """
   234	            SELECT COALESCE(MAX(CAST(SUBSTR(event_id, 5) AS INTEGER)), 0)
   235	            FROM event
   236	            WHERE event_id GLOB 'evt-[0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]'
   237	            """
   238	        ).fetchone()
   239	        return int(row[0] or 0)
   240

222	class TestCrashRecoveryRollsBackPartialWrites:
   223	    """§3.3 + §6.3: a transaction that fails must leave no partial state."""
   224	
   225	    def test_exception_during_commit_rolls_back(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   226	        """A failure inside ``_apply_commit`` MUST leave nothing persisted.
   227	
   228	        We monkey-patch ``_insert_event`` to raise on the second event
   229	        of a composite commit (``create_implement_task`` emits two
   230	        events: ``task.created`` + ``proposal.dispatched``). If
   231	        rollback works, a reopened store sees neither the task
   232	        row nor the proposal state change — everything stays at the
   233	        pre-operation checkpoint.
   234	        """
   235	        path = tmp_path / "eden.db"
   236	        first = SqliteStore("exp-r", path, token_factory=_token_seq())
   237	        first.create_proposal(
   238	            Proposal(
   239	                proposal_id="p1",
   240	                experiment_id="exp-r",
   241	                slug="feat-a",
   242	                priority=1.0,
   243	                parent_commits=["a" * 40],
   244	                artifacts_uri="https://artifacts.example/p1",
   245	                state="drafting",
   246	                created_at="2026-04-23T00:00:00.000Z",
   247	            )
   248	        )
   249	        first.mark_proposal_ready("p1")
   250	        checkpoint_events = [e.event_id for e in first.events()]
   251	
   252	        calls = {"n": 0}
   253	        original = first._insert_event  # noqa: SLF001
   254	
   255	        def boom(event):  # noqa: ANN001, ANN202
   256	            calls["n"] += 1
   257	            if calls["n"] == 2:
   258	                raise RuntimeError("simulated mid-commit failure")
   259	            return original(event)
   260	
   261	        first._insert_event = boom  # noqa: SLF001
   262	        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="simulated"):
   263	            first.create_implement_task("t-impl", "p1")
   264	        first._insert_event = original  # noqa: SLF001
   265	        first.close()
   266	
   267	        # Reopen and verify: no task row, proposal still 'ready', event
   268	        # log unchanged from the checkpoint.
   269	        second = SqliteStore("exp-r", path)
   270	        assert second.list_tasks() == []
   271	        assert second.read_proposal("p1").state == "ready"
   272	        assert [e.event_id for e in second.events()] == checkpoint_events
   273	        second.close()
   274

item.text:
**Blockers**
- The `Store` Protocol does not faithfully encode chapter 8 as claimed. [protocol.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/protocol.py:53) exposes workflow-specific methods like `create_plan_task()`/`create_implement_task()`/`create_evaluate_task()` and a single `events()` snapshot, but chapter 8 normatively names `create_task`, `read_range`, `subscribe`, and `replay`, and also defines an artifact-store contract. See [08-storage.md §1.1](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/08-storage.md:35), [§2.1](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/08-storage.md:136), and [§5.1](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/08-storage.md:274). The repo explicitly declares artifact storage a non-goal in [README.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/README.md:33), so “pins the chapter-8 union” in [roadmap.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/roadmap.md:195) and [0.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/review/phase-6-storage/impl/20260423T163433/0.md:171) overstates what shipped. A cleaner reading would be either separate `TaskStore`/`EventLog`/`ArtifactStore` protocols plus a façade, or a spec-shaped single protocol that still includes the event-log and artifact-store operations.
- `SqliteStore`’s durability setting does not satisfy a literal reading of `08-storage.md §3.1`’s “crash of the store’s host”. The code sets WAL + `synchronous=NORMAL` in [sqlite.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/sqlite.py:161), and the brief/roadmap claim that this satisfies §3.1 in [0.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/review/phase-6-storage/impl/20260423T163433/0.md:95) and [roadmap.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/roadmap.md:201). SQLite’s own docs say WAL+NORMAL remains consistent but “does lose durability”; committed transactions may roll back after power loss or system crash, while WAL+FULL adds the per-commit sync for durability: https://www.sqlite.org/pragma.html#pragma_synchronous
- `_max_event_seq()` is not just future-fragile; it is already wrong once the default counter reaches `evt-1000000`. The default generator emits `evt-{n:06d}` in [_base.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/_base.py:175), but [sqlite.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/sqlite.py:232) only scans exactly six digits. I reproduced this locally: after persisting `evt-1000000`, reopen seeds from `999999`, and the next append fails with `UNIQUE constraint failed: event.event_id`. That makes the “resumes the counter from the persisted max” claim false in [0.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/review/phase-6-storage/impl/20260423T163433/0.md:104) and [roadmap.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/roadmap.md:205).
- Metrics-schema immutability is enforced as literal JSON-string equality, which is stricter than the spec’s semantic “schema MUST NOT be mutated” rule. [sqlite.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/sqlite.py:193) serializes the schema and compares `stored_schema != schema_json` at [sqlite.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/sqlite.py:215). Because JSON object key order is not semantically meaningful here, reopening with the same metric map in a different insertion order is rejected, even though the schema did not mutate under [08-storage.md §4.2](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/08-storage.md:235). I reproduced that false rejection locally.

**Assessment**
The shared `_StoreBase` design is solid, and I did not find a current partial-visibility bug in the composite-commit paths themselves. I ran `uv run pytest -q reference/packages/eden-storage/tests`; all 119 tests passed. The main issue is that the implementation and docs over-claim chapter-8 conformance: atomic rollback looks good, but the public contract, durability setting, and reopen semantics are not fully aligned with the spec.

**Non-Blocker Suggestions**
- Atomicity looks correct for current composite commits. `_apply_commit()` runs inside one explicit SQLite transaction in [sqlite.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/sqlite.py:379), and the injected mid-commit failure test in [test_restart_safety.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/tests/test_restart_safety.py:225) cleanly rolls back. I could not construct a current sequence where a partial composite commit becomes visible after an exception; the real issue is durability under host crash, not atomicity.
- The `_in_txn` re-entrancy model is acceptable today but discipline-based. [sqlite.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/sqlite.py:260) protects nested helper calls in SQLite, but [memory.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/memory.py:49) has no rollback layer. If a future refactor re-enters public methods or calls `_apply_commit()` before all validation is finished, SQLite could mask the mistake while the in-memory backend would not. A stricter “stage first, single commit point second” structure would remove that class of risk.
- The brief has a few accuracy issues beyond the blockers. “Monkey-patched commit failure verifies rollback” in [0.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/review/phase-6-storage/impl/20260423T163433/0.md:124) and [CLAUDE.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/CLAUDE.md:29) is overstated; the test injects a failure during `_apply_commit`, not during SQLite `COMMIT`. “`ensure_schema` applies every pending one under a single outer transaction” in [0.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/review/phase-6-storage/impl/20260423T163433/0.md:100) is also overstated; [_schema.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/_schema.py:93) never issues `BEGIN`, and `executescript()` under autocommit is not one outer migration transaction.
- I did not find remaining mutable-reference leaks in the public read/list/events/submission boundary. The deep-copy and JSON rehydration story in [_base.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/_base.py:269) and [sqlite.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/sqlite.py:295) looks complete.
- Event retention and no-fabrication are mostly fine in practice: I found no deletion/compaction path, and persisted entities/events come from explicit caller-facing operations. The missing chapter-8 behavior is API surface, not hidden background behavior: `replay`/`subscribe` are absent, and artifact-store §5 is absent entirely.
